texts.full_text: \[[@B1-ijms-21-03410],[@B2-ijms-21-03410]\]. The disease has a prevalence of approximately 1 in 50,000 people in European populations \[[@B3-ijms-21-03410]\], with an early onset between 10 and 15 years, and most patients normally become wheelchair users around 15 years after the beginning of the symptoms \[[@B4-ijms-21-03410]\]. FRDA, firstly described by physician Nicolas Friedreich in 1861, is caused by the reduction or absence of frataxin, a protein that is widely expressed in all tissues, with the highest levels in the heart, skeletal muscle, brain, and spinal cord \[[@B5-ijms-21-03410],[@B6-ijms-21-03410]\]. Frataxin plays a crucial role in multiple cellular processes, such as mitochondrial iron homeostasis, energy production, and oxidative stress response \[[@B7-ijms-21-03410],[@B8-ijms-21-03410]\]. In FRDA, the frataxin gene () is affected by the expansion o

texts.full_text: aimed to investigate the role of estrogen on GATA-3 expression and the relationshipbetween its regulation and Th2 cytokine production in endometrial cells derived from EM patients. The findings suggest that elevated levels of estrogen may promote increased expression of GATA-3, subsequently leading to an imbalance in immune response favoring a more inflammatory environment due to the upregulation of Th2 cytokines such as IL-4 and IL-10. This dysregulated local hormone/cytokine microenvironment could contribute significantly to endometriotic lesion development and progression by fostering chronic inflammation, pain perception, and possibly affecting disease severity in EM

texts.full_text: to act as one unified bloc when engaging with the January primates' meeting invitation extended by Archbishop Welby for Canterbury. Archbishops from these regions have expressed concerns about what they perceive as an imposition of liberal social agendas, particularly on issues related to sexuality and marriage equality within their respective churches. The Episcopal Church’s ordination of a gay bishop in 2015 sparked significant tensions across the Anglican Communion, with many conservative primates expressing disapproval or even threatening schism if such practices continued unabated by liberal congregations from more progressive regions. In response to Mr Gjelten’s query about "ideological colonization," Archbishop Welby acknowledged these concerns but emphasized the need for dialogue and unity within diversity, stating
